conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
That's great! So you're saying the commute was good this week? What about your work as a chef? Any exciting projects or events coming up?
Yeah, work's been keeping me busy, always something new to prepare. Honestly, I'm just trying to keep my head above water.
I understand. It sounds like you're feeling a bit overwhelmed. How are you spending your free time, if any?
Free time? Ha! What's that? (laughs) Seriously though, when I do have some downtime, I've been watching cooking shows and trying new recipes.
That sounds like a great way to unwind! Are there any friends or family members you've been spending quality time with lately?
Yeah, my sister and I have been having Sunday dinner together. We catch up on each other's lives.
That's lovely. How has your sleep been? Any issues getting restful sleep?
Sleep's been... sketchy. Lately, I've been lying awake for hours in the middle of the night.
I see. Do you think there might be something going on at work that's keeping you up at night?
Yeah, maybe. My boss has been breathing down my neck about some new menu changes. It's stressing me out.
That can be tough to deal with. How's your energy level been? Have you noticed any differences in how you're feeling physically?
Energy-wise, I'm just drained all the time. Like a battery running low on power.

I understand. It sounds like you might be feeling pretty exhausted. Can you tell me more about what's going on with your appetite lately? Are you eating regularly or having trouble digesting certain foods?
Appetite's been nonexistent, to be honest. Even the thought of cooking for myself makes me tired.
That can be really tough. How has your social life been affected by all this? Have you had to cancel plans with friends or family recently?
Yeah, I've had to turn down a few invitations. It's hard to muster up the energy to go out when you're feeling so down.
That makes sense. It sounds like you're feeling pretty withdrawn right now. Are there any hobbies or activities that bring you joy and take your mind off things?
Honestly, I used to love cooking for fun, but now it feels more like a chore than a passion. Even thinking about it exhausts me.
I see. How's your concentration been lately? Have you noticed any difficulties focusing or making decisions?
Concentration-wise, I'm all over the place. One minute I'm focused on something, and the next, my mind is wandering off in a million directions.
That can be really frustrating. Are there any specific times of day when your energy seems to dip the most?
Yeah, definitely after lunch. It's like my batteries get drained even further.
I see. Have you noticed any changes in your mood lately? Do you feel like you're stuck in a rut or feeling hopeless?
Yeah, it feels like there's this dark cloud following me around everywhere. Nothing seems to lift my spirits anymore.
I understand. It sounds like you're struggling with some pretty heavy feelings right now. Can you tell me more about what's been going on recently that might be contributing to these feelings?
Well, my sister's been dealing with some health issues lately, and it's got me worried sick.
I'm so sorry to hear that. That can be really tough to deal with. How have you been coping with the stress of having a family member going through a difficult time?
Honestly, I've just been trying to put on a brave face and be strong for my sister. But it's hard when it feels like everything is falling apart.
That makes sense. It sounds like you're shouldering a lot of responsibility right now. Have you thought about seeking out some support from friends or family members?
Yeah, I've been trying to reach out to my friends more often, but it's hard when you don't feel like talking.
I understand. Sometimes it can be helpful to talk things through with someone who isn't directly affected by the situation. Would that be something you'd consider?
Maybe... I don't know if anyone would really understand what I'm going through.

That's understandable. But sometimes just having someone to listen and offer support can make a big difference. What do you think your goals are for our time together today? What would you like to work on or get out of this therapy?
Honestly, I just want to feel like myself again. I miss the person I used to be before all this started.
That's completely understandable. It sounds like you're feeling really lost and disconnected from your usual self. Can we work towards getting you back in touch with that person?
